description_long: This is a rectangular manhole cover set within a matching rectangular section of dark, coarse asphalt pavement. The cover itself is made of heavy cast iron, featuring a grid of raised rectangular and square patterns for traction and structural integrity. The edges of the cover are slightly beveled, and there are subtle blue paint markings at the corners, possibly for utility identification. The surface is weathered, showing signs of use and exposure to the elements, with a matte, slightly rough finish. No visible bolts, fasteners, or manufacturer logos are present, and the cover sits flush with the surrounding pavement.
description: A rectangular cast iron manhole cover with a grid pattern, set in dark asphalt and marked with blue paint at the corners.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full rectangular grid pattern and blue corner markings on the manhole cover.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the beveled edge and the depth of the grid pattern, with the blue markings more visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the grid pattern and the flush fit with the surrounding asphalt.
description_view_3: Angled view from the other end, emphasizing the rectangular shape and the weathered surface texture.
attribute_pattern_design: Rectangular grid of raised rectangles and squares, with subtle blue paint markings at the corners.
